content:
4 min read
Just now

--

Once a week our studio holds a “viewing” — a teardown of a recent project. Usually praise and complaints land about evenly. But the review of the Energoline site went strangely: there was almost nothing to pick at. “The cleanest and most thoroughly considered project of the recent batch,” our art director said. It also came together faster than other projects of a similar scope. Here’s how — honestly, including what didn’t work.

The Client
Energoline supplies equipment for the power industry, specializing in gas turbine cooling. For scale: one cooling system is essentially an air conditioner the size of a two- or three-story house. They supply, modernize, and service these systems.
A detail that explains a lot: power equipment in Russia is historically European and American, and importing it has gotten harder. Energoline runs a worldwide network of legal entities for that. But you can’t lean on it forever, so the company is shifting focus from “buy it and ship it” toward engineering and in-house expertise. The site had to show that pivot — and they had no website at all, so we started from zero.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

What the Client Brought, and Why It’s Half the Win
Energoline arrived with a finished brand book: logo, guide, an orange brand color. But assets are only half of it. The bigger thing: the client listened and trusted us, didn’t change requirements mid-flight, didn’t set fires around deadlines, and assembled the content for each complex technical direction themselves. Without that, we’d have drowned in the subject matter.
The takeaway for anyone commissioning digital work: come with your materials, trust the team, and don’t start a fire where there isn’t one. It directly affects speed and quality.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

An Honest Word on AI: It Accelerates, It Doesn’t Decide
Here’s the unpopular opinion. We used AI at every stage — renders, ideas, code, markup. It genuinely saves days. But AI speeds work up; it doesn’t replace it. And it rests on two things.
First, a quality design file broken into tokens, styles, and components. Then a human and an AI read it the same way, and code generation turns from a lottery into a predictable process. AI won’t save a bad file — it’ll just multiply the mess faster.
Second, finishing by hand. AI never hands you a finished result — it always needs finishing “by hand” until the picture feels pleasant, individual, and evokes the right emotion. That’s exactly what a studio is hired for. Those details separate “generated” from “made.”

Why, in the Age of AI, a Trained Eye Decides
An uncomfortable truth: AI today is equally available to everyone. The same tool sits in our hands, in the hands of a studio assembled yesterday, and even in the client’s own team. The difference isn’t the tool — it’s who’s driving it.
AI produces dozens of options. Choosing the one that works takes a trained eye: feeling where a composition falls apart, where render lighting looks cheap, where typography drifts. You can’t google or generate that — it’s earned over years. We have 16 years of it, specifically in image-driven solutions and visualizations. That’s what turns “AI + a structured file + finishing by hand” into a result that doesn’t look like a template.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

A Bit of Tech
Astro on the frontend, separated front and back, connected over a REST API. Direction pages aren’t three separate templates but assemble from block components the client can rearrange themselves. PageSpeed: 100 on desktop, 96–98 on mobile before analytics.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

What Didn’t Work
Honestly: we had to pull the infographics from the “About” block — the client couldn’t settle on the numbers, and the block ended up a little bare. A separate page with the human stories (they have their own hockey team) is parked for now. That’s the part we’d still like to finish.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Takeaway
“Fast” and “beautiful” aren’t mutually exclusive when a structured file, AI on the routine, finishing by hand, and a trained eye all line up. But the real multiplier is a reasonable client. With one, a project turns out the way this one did.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Chipsa, chipsa.design — we build sites that load fast and look like someone thought about them. Because someone did — for 16 years running.
